title: 1.1: Reports that Scapy extracts the embedded packet from ICMP errors (e.g., shows IPerror layer present, shows ICMPerror layer created)
1.2: Reports that Scapy uses fields from the embedded packet to determine matching (e.g., shows IPerror src equals 192.168.1.100, shows IPerror dst equals 192.0.2.1, shows IPerror ID equals 12345)
1.3: Reports the hash value generated for the test packet (e.g., 00a803650164000100).
1.4: Reports that the embedded packet hash equals the original request hash (e.g., shows Embedded packet hash 00a803650164000100, shows Hashes match equals True).
1.5: Reports that changing TTL in the embedded packet does not break matching (e.g., Modified TTL 64 to 1 still gives Matches equals 1).
1.6: Reports that changing checksum in the embedded packet does not break matching (e.g., Modified checksum to 0xFFFF still gives Matches equals 1).
1.7: Reports that checkIPsrc=True causes an embedded packet with different source IP to fail matching (e.g., wrong source 10.0.0.1 gives Matches equals 0)
1.8: Reports that checkIPsrc=False allows an embedded packet with different source IP to match (e.g., wrong source 10.0.0.1 gives Matches equals 1)
1.9: Reports that check_TCPerror_seqack=False allows an embedded TCP packet with different sequence number to match (e.g., Modified TCP seq 1000000 to 9999999 gives Matches equals 1)

1.10: Reports that check_TCPerror_seqack=True causes an embedded TCP packet with different sequence number to fail matching (e.g., Modified TCP seq with check_TCPerror_seqack=True gives Matches equals 0)

1.11: Reports that changing the configuration settings affects which packets Scapy considers valid responses (e.g., toggling checkIPsrc changes Matches from 0 to 1, toggling check_TCPerror_seqack changes Matches from 1 to 0)
1.12: Reports the trade-off between strict validation versus tolerating network variations (e.g., strict setting reduces false positives, lenient setting improves compatibility with real routers)
1.13: Reports that byte-order tolerance applies when conf.checkIPID=True".
1.14: Reports that the numeric transformation uses socket.htons() byte-swapping (e.g., 0x1234 becomes 0x3412 in the accepted ID).

1.15: Reports that loading Scapy extension parsing for RFC 4884 data does not change matching behavior (e.g., extended ICMP error with additional bytes still gives Matches equals 1, hash equals 00a803650164000100)
2.1: Claims Scapy performs full byte-by-byte packet comparison for matching (e.g., states entire packet must be identical, claims all fields must match exactly)
2.2: Claims byte-swap tolerance applies regardless of configuration settings (e.g., states byte-swapped IDs always accepted, claims socket.htons() works unconditionally)
